headlines.title: Dutch PM unveils Moluccan monument marking dark colonial chapter
headlines.snippet: Dutch Prime Minister Rob Jetten unveiled a memorial for the Moluccan community. The monument commemorates decades of mistreatment that the community endured.

headlines.title: Our life stops’: West Bank childhood shattered by Israeli military raids
headlines.snippet: A UN report details the severe impact of Israeli military raids on Palestinian children. The report documents killings, detentions, and lasting psychological harm.

headlines.title: Ivan Cepeda concedes defeat in Colombia election, sealing right-wing win
headlines.snippet: Presidential candidate Ivan Cepeda has conceded defeat in the Colombian election. His opponent Abelardo de la Espriella has secured the right-wing victory.

headlines.title: Palestinian boy amongst dead in Israeli strike on displaced tent in Gaza
headlines.snippet: An Israeli drone strike on a tent sheltering displaced Palestinians in Khan Younis resulted in two fatalities. Among the deceased was a child.

headlines.title: Flash floods hit India’s Arunachal Pradesh
headlines.snippet: Torrential rainfall in India's northeastern state of Arunachal Pradesh has triggered flash floods and landslides.

headlines.title: UN report no air strikes in Lebanon for 16 hours despite Israeli military activity
headlines.snippet: UN peacekeepers in southern Lebanon recorded no air strikes or missile launches between midnight and 4 PM local time on Tuesday. However, they did observe two missile trajectories overnight.

headlines.title: Iran rejects Rubio's claims on regional instability
headlines.snippet: Iran has rejected US Secretary of State Marco Rubio's claims blaming Tehran-backed groups for regional instability. Iran argues that US policy and Israeli military actions are the primary drivers of conflict.

headlines.title: Rubio says Gulf talks were 'frank' as US seeks regional backing
headlines.snippet: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio stated that discussions with Gulf leaders regarding regional issues and the US-Iran agreement were candid. He indicated that Washington sought input from its allies.

headlines.title: UN: RSF used rape and sexual slavery as weapons of war in Sudan since 2023
headlines.snippet: A UN report indicates that the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) paramilitary group in Sudan has been responsible for the majority of sexual crimes since 2023. These acts may amount to crimes against humanity.

headlines.title: Rubio rejects proposal to charge ships for using Strait of Hormuz
headlines.snippet: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio has dismissed the idea of charging ships for using the Strait of Hormuz. He stated that Washington would oppose any such mechanism for passage through the international waterway.

headlines.title: Federal judge bars Trump from implementing proof of citizenship requirement to vote
headlines.snippet: A federal judge has issued an order preventing Trump from implementing a proof-of-citizenship requirement for voting.

headlines.snippet: The US has reopened its embassy in Kuwait following an Iranian attack several months prior.

headlines.title: Yemen rejects Israeli claims of Houthi plot against Eilat

headlines.snippet: A humanitarian crisis is imminent in El-Obeid as RSF paramilitaries advance. There are significant fears of atrocities if the city is captured.

headlines.snippet: Abubakr Nanabawa, founder of Muslim Vote, urged Burnham to reject genocide and prioritize the UK. He noted the challenges Labour faces in regaining Muslim voter support.
